channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Even as drones usher in an era of ‘cheap kill,’ Army leaders look to what’s next | Defense One
item.description: Uncrewed vehicles were everywhere at LANPAC—even above a general’s head.

item.title: 🌏 Mysterious Chinese SUV With Massive Roof Featured In Trump Motorcade In Beijing | The War Zone
item.description: The bizarre-looking high-top SUVs were among several notable vehicles spotted in President Trump's motorcade during his recent trip to China. The post Mysterious Chinese SUV With Massive Roof Featured In Trump Motorcade In Beijing appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army’s 7th Infantry Division, 1st MDTF to merge as Multi-Domain Command-Pacific | Defense News
item.description: The two-star Multi-Domain Command-Pacific will merge the 7th ID’s Stryker infantry brigades and a combat aviation brigade with a multidomain task force.

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: The water tastes metallic. The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Where’s the beef? Trump’s underwhelming meeting with Xi. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Trump appears to have sidestepped the biggest trap—Beijing’s attempt to box Washington in on Taiwan—but underperformed on everything else. The post Where’s the beef? Trump’s underwhelming meeting with Xi. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 What the Trump-Xi Summit Won't Solve | Foreign Affairs
item.description: A conversation with Orville Schell.

item.title: 📍 The problem with the US power-sharing plan for Libya | Atlantic Council
item.description: The nascent US initiative may succeed in reducing tensions and creating a slightly more predictable environment for foreign businesses, but a structural transformation remains out of reach. The post The problem with the US power-sharing plan for Libya appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🌍 Braw in Sky News on Iran threatening tariffs on undersea cables | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 15, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw was interviewed by Sky News on Iran threatening to place tariffs on vital undersea cables that support global connectivity. The post Braw in Sky News on Iran threatening tariffs on undersea cables appeared…

item.title: 📍 Don’t fall for the middle-power mirage | Atlantic Council
item.description: The kind of multilateralism that Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney spoke about in Davos remains an aspiration rather than an achievable reality. The post Don’t fall for the middle-power mirage appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 HASC leader threatens Pentagon with ‘pain’ over canceled Europe deployment | Defense One
item.description: Recent Trump-administration moves may drop troop presence below legal minimums.

item.title: 🕵️ CIA Director Ratcliffe’s Trip to Havana | Foreign Policy
item.description: The United States is offering to turn Cuba’s lights back on—for a price.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army to receive thousands of Barracuda-500M cruise missiles in Anduril deal | Defense News
item.description: The Pentagon’s Low-Cost Containerized Missiles program aims to obtain over 10,000 low-cost cruise missiles over a three-year span.

item.title: 📍 Argentina Retires Its A-4 Fightinghawks | The War Zone
item.description: After six decades of service, the A-4’s first export operator has finally stood down its Skyhawks as it introduces its first F-16s. The post Argentina Retires Its A-4 Fightinghawks appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of May 9: U.S. President Donald Trump visits China, India and Kenya host summits, and several prime ministers have a rough time.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth memo calls for sweeping, open-ended review of Pentagon's legal system | Defense One
item.description: It’s the secretary's latest unusual move toward DOD’s military and civilian lawyers.

item.title: 🏴 Army leaders in hot seat over Poland deployment cancellation | Defense News
item.description: Lawmakers questioned the timing and the reasons, lambasting the order that Rep. Don Bacon, R-Neb., said sent a “terrible message to Russia and our allies.”

item.title: 📍 A ‘Lord of the Flies’ for Our Time | Foreign Policy
item.description: This is the perfect project for the creator of ‘Adolescence.’

item.title: 📍 The 156,000 and the 1.4 Billion | Foreign Policy
item.description: What Curaçao figured out about World Cup soccer that India still hasn’t.

item.title: 🏛️ No sound of silence: US soldiers train eyes — and ears — for drone swarms | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Army is moving beyond battling individual drone threats as it experiments with tactics to combat throngs of unmanned aircraft in saturated skies.

item.title: 📍 AIRO’s ‘slowed rotor’ hybrid-electric VTOL drone aims to solve resupply issues | Breaking Defense
item.description: Company executives told Breaking Defense the dual-use design targets a logistics gap between rear-operational hubs and forward units.

item.title: 📍 War Is an International Crime. Why Does It Go Unpunished? | Foreign Policy
item.description: A sweeping legal history reveals how the international community failed to live up to the promises of Nuremberg.

item.title: 🏛️ USS Gerald R. Ford to return from 11-month deployment on Saturday | Defense News
item.description: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is finally returning home after etching its name into Navy history books.

item.title: 🏴 I Was the Russian Commander in a War Game. This Is How I Defeated NATO. | Foreign Policy
item.description: Decision paralysis and divisions among alliance members were easy to exploit.

item.title: ⚛️ Secretive AIM-260 Air-To-Air Missile Finally Breaks Cover | The War Zone
item.description: The AIM-260 has been in development for years as a much-needed longer-range successor to the venerable AIM-120 AMRAAM. The post Secretive AIM-260 Air-To-Air Missile Finally Breaks Cover appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Inside Ukraine’s Interceptor Drone Innovations Swatting Down Thousands Of Russian Shaheds | The War Zone
item.description: Brave1 CEO Andrii Hrytseniuk gives us exclusive insights into Ukraine's ability to counter Russia's one-way attack drone armada on the cheap. The post Inside Ukraine’s Interceptor Drone Innovations Swatting Down Thousands Of Russian Shaheds appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon informed Army ‘just a couple of days ago’ on decision to halt Poland deployment | Breaking Defense
item.description: “This was all relatively recent, and we worked back and forth on what those recommendations would be, and the order came down,” Acting Army Chief of Staff Gen. Christopher LaNeve said.

item.title: 🏴 The UK faces a dilemma over potential troop deployments to Ukraine. France can help. | Atlantic Council
item.description: France is currently punching below its weight in the Baltic area and could shoulder more of the burden. The post The UK faces a dilemma over potential troop deployments to Ukraine. France can help. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 If President Trump is Concerned With the Entry of “Criminal Aliens,” Why Is the U.S. Welcoming Corrupt Foreign Officials? | Just Security
item.description: A former justice minister from Poland who is wanted there on abuse of power charges has somehow turned up in the United States. The post If President Trump is Concerned With the Entry of “Criminal Aliens,” Why Is the U.S. Welcoming Corrupt Foreign Officials? appeared first on…

item.title: 🌏 What did Trump and Xi accomplish? | Atlantic Council
item.description: On Friday, the US president concluded talks with his counterpart from China, soaring away in Air Force One after two days of meetings and ceremonies. Our experts shed light on the outcomes. The post What did Trump and Xi accomplish? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🛡️ Canada-led brigade in Latvia moves beyond tripwire role, commander says | Defense News
item.description: “Right now I have a brigade, there is nothing on the other side of the border that can take out this brigade,” the unit commander told Defense News.
